adversarial_check.description: Two-part check beyond the 16 listed commands. (a) Simulated a python3-less environment: built a minimal PATH directory containing only symlinks to bash, git, and coreutils (basename/cat/cp/date/dirname/mkdir/mktemp/mv/rm/sh/env) -- explicitly no python3 -- then ran 'env -i PATH=<minimal_bin> bash bootstrap.sh --verify-only --tag v0.1.0 --target <nonexistent-target>'. It failed immediately with 'required command python3 not found on PATH' and exit 1, confirming the new require_command python3 guard fires before the target-directory-exists check and before check_nizam_index/check_provenance_tag's python3 heredoc invocations, eliminating the misleading crash the fix targets. (b) Re-validated all 24 shipped .md frontmatter blocks (parsed as YAML) against schema/frontmatter.schema.json via python3+jsonschema, and separately asserted each file's authoritative_source value exactly equals its own repo-relative path -- all 24 pass both checks with zero mismatches and zero stale prefixes.
